多年来，你可能还记得，我们会写到在论坛上寻找专利巨魔的疯狂本质，在这种情况下，巨魔会成群结队地涌向东得克萨斯州的联邦法院。回溯到近15年前，我们写道，东得克萨斯州法院如何成为专利流氓案件的主要中心，导致了各种粗略的行为。在德克萨斯州的小城市(主要是马歇尔和泰勒)，有一堆空置的写字楼只是为了假装在那里有办公室。参与许多专利案件的公司开始试图讨好这些小城市的居民，以防他们可能是陪审团成员。就在陪审团即将对TiVo巨魔案做出裁决的几周前，TiVo真的买下了一辆“大冠军牛”(Grand Champion Steer&34；34；)。三星在当地的Stagecoach Days活动上投入了如此多的资金，以至于将其更名为三星Stagecoach Days，并在马歇尔法院旁边建造了一个三星溜冰场。
多年来，人们一直向国会施压，要求国会解决这一烂摊子，但相反，最高法院最终介入，做出了TC哈特兰的裁决，并表示适当的管辖权应该是被告实际成立公司的地方。当然，这似乎产生了相反的效果--因为公司不再想留在德克萨斯州东部。苹果关闭了那里的门店，以规避管辖权。
当然，如果你认为评委们会安静地离开，那你就大错特错了。东得克萨斯州的一些法官总是觉得他们喜欢他们建立起来的对专利巨魔超级友好的声誉。有一段时间是T·约翰·沃德法官。当他离开法官席(当然是成为一名专利律师)时，罗德尼·吉尔斯特拉普法官填补了他留下的空白。他甚至试图无视最高法院对TC心脏地带的裁决(尽管联邦巡回上诉法院对此不以为然)。
然而，正如“专利进展”所指出的，现在有一位新法官在争夺专利流氓圣诞节名单的首位，而他就在西德克萨斯州。法官艾伦·奥尔布赖特曾是一名专利诉讼律师，于2018年被任命为法官--他实际上是在一次巡回旅行中说服公司在他的法院提起专利诉讼：
美国地区法官艾伦·奥尔布赖特(Alan Albright)和律师去年预测韦科的联邦法院将成为专利和知识产权诉讼的温床，但他们的预测略有失误。
随着奥尔布赖特在全国各地游说商业和专利律师，宣传韦科公司的新联邦法官(一名长期担任专利诉讼律师)将提供专业知识，在韦科公司创造一个高效和友好的环境，过去一年的反应实际上超出了预期。
自2018年9月奥尔布赖特上任以来，包括韦科在内的德克萨斯州联邦西区已经提交了250多起专利案件。这一总数超过了前四年的总和，使西区成为全国专利案件最繁忙的地区之一。
我很难读到这些而不去想..。那真是一团糟。法官不应该走遍全国招揽生意。选择论坛是一个糟糕的主意，这是有原因的--更糟糕的是：它的论坛销售。正如那篇文章指出的那样，越来越多的专利律师事务所现在正在奥尔布赖特法院附近的韦科开展业务，甚至连东得克萨斯州的巨魔律师事务所也在向西移动：
韦科律师事务所Haley&amp；Olson在3月份宣布，它正在与曼恩、廷德尔和汤普森律师事务所(一家专门从事专利诉讼的东得克萨斯州律师事务所)联手，以利用韦科联邦法院的新焦点。
这应该被视为一个问题，但相反，德克萨斯州的联邦法院似乎一切如常。
两位法学教授现在提出了一篇新的论文，深入研究了这一令人不安的趋势，文章的开头是一则相当有说服力的模仿Craigslist的广告，他们说这令人震惊……。准确地描述了美国德克萨斯州西区地区法院目前正在发生的事情。
J·乔纳斯·安德森(J.Jonas Anderson)和保罗·古格里扎(Paul Gugliuzza)这两位教授相当直截了当地阐述了这些问题：
一名法官在不到两年前被任命为法院法官，他一直在宣传他的选区-通过向有资质的律师介绍情况，向媒体发表评论，他的法庭程序，以及专利案件的裁决-作为提起专利侵权诉讼的地方。他已经成功了。2018年，西区仅收到90件专利案件-仅占全国专利诉讼的2.5%。2020年，西区的接待人数将超过800人，是全国接待人数最多的地区。重要的是，这些诉讼绝大多数是由所谓的专利流氓提起的-这些实体不生产任何产品或提供服务，而只是为了执行专利而存在。
专利案件集中在一名法官面前，完全由他自己采取行动寻求专利诉讼，这得益于西区的立案制度，该制度允许原告不仅可以选择法院，还可以选择将审理他们案件的具体法官。这些动态-判决专利案件的广告和原告为该法官购物-破坏了公众对司法公正的信心，使法院成为诉讼当事人的不公平竞争环境，并促进了专利流氓对诉讼的支持。
正如他们所指出的，如果国会有意愿，这似乎是一件很容易解决的事情：
两项改革将有助于解决这个问题：第一，法律规定，地区法官应该随机分配到案件中；第二，专利案件的地点应该与司法区内的地理分区挂钩，而不仅仅是整个地区。
这篇文章甚至详细描述了在大流行期间，奥尔布赖特法官和吉尔斯特拉普法官似乎都在争先恐后地亲自处理尽可能多的专利案件，以便有更多的巨魔在他们的法院提起诉讼：
新冠肺炎大流行颠覆了美国人的生活。数百万人失业。许多仍然有工作的人-杂货店工人，送货司机，医疗保健提供者-每天都在冒着生命危险。我们这些享有特权的人已经学会了完全在家工作；我们最大的兴趣是在Zoom会议的背景下漫步的跳舞的孩子或流浪的宠物。COVID也扰乱了法律实践。即使是美国最高法院也做出了让步，通过电话举行口头辩论，并首次现场直播。美国联邦巡回上诉法院审理全国范围内所有专利案件的上诉，也同样暂停了面对面的口头辩论，直到另行通知。
但奥尔布赖特法官似乎决心一如既往地推进专利诉讼。其他专利繁多的地区法院的法官也是如此。尽管一些法官妥协并在Zoom案上进行了专利诉讼，但德克萨斯州东区首席法官罗德尼·吉尔斯特拉普(Rodney Gilstrap)直到去年还比全国任何一名法官审理的专利案件都多，最近他拒绝了被告提出的推迟原定于8月3日开始的亲自陪审团审判的动议，引用诗人罗伯特·弗罗斯特(Robert Frost)的成语：“最好的出路总是通过。”这些评委怎么了？
他们正处于一场激烈的竞争中，以吸引专利案件进入他们的法庭。正如这篇文章所显示的，奥尔布赖特法官正在获胜。而这种法庭竞争对专利系统或更广泛的法院系统都是不利的。
正如文章指出的那样，这次论坛抛售是历史论坛选购出人意料的另一面。奥尔布赖特法官似乎已经想出了如何以前所未有的方式说服他的法院。他甚至会做的一件事是让专利持有者在他位于韦科的法院起诉，将案件移交给奥斯汀更方便的法院……。但仍让奥尔布赖特法官主持此案。下面是一个简单的图表，说明了这一点在实践中是如何发挥作用的：
再说一次，几乎所有这些案件都将提交给一名法官。如果你不认为这是个问题，那么你很可能是个专利流氓。奥尔布赖特法官对此也不是特别害羞：
2018年被任命为地区法官后，奥尔布赖特立即进行了媒体闪电战，让所有人都知道他的法院欢迎专利诉讼。“韦科论坛报-先驱报”报道说，奥尔布赖特法官“毫不含糊地让人们知道，他希望自己的韦科法庭成为知识产权案件的中心。”他出席了专利诉讼律师和专利拥有者的晚宴，赞扬在韦科审理专利案件的好处。奥尔布赖特法官表示，他之所以在韦科担任这一职位，是因为他“认为这是一个完美的地方，可以尝试并为复杂的专利诉讼建立一个严肃的场所。”最能说明问题的是，他在美国知识产权法协会2019年年会上做了一次演讲，题为“为什么你应该在‘HeySugar’对面的街道上提交你的下一个专利案件”，他指的是他的韦科法院附近的一家糖果店。
你会认为法官会意识到这看起来有多糟糕，但我想不会。
正如这份文件详细描述的那样，奥尔布赖特法官是韦科唯一的法官，所以如果你在那里提起诉讼，你肯定会得到他，而且他明确表示，他将亲自处理专利案件的所有细节，将其他案件的繁琐工作外包给地方法官：
由于知道在韦科提交的专利案件将由奥尔布赖特法官审理，专利原告甚至更有动力在韦科提交申请，因为奥尔布赖特法官向他的地方法官发出了独特的转让命令。奥尔布赖特法官将所有案件都指派给他的地方法官处理所有非处分动议--专利、版权和某些人身保护令案件除外。因此，专利原告知道，奥尔布赖特法官将亲自参与诉讼的每一个方面，不会被他要处理的其他非专利案件分心。地区法官在诉讼的所有阶段都给予如此高度的关注是极其罕见的。例如，特拉华州在专利案件中大量使用地方法官。即使是德克萨斯州东区也依赖地方法官来处理重要的动议和预审听证会，包括索赔解释。
对于原告来说，选择韦科分部再简单不过了，原告只需从西区电子立案系统的分部下拉菜单中选择“韦科”，案件就会自动分配给奥尔布赖特法官。
这篇论文接着详述了越来越多疯狂的例子，说明这一切是多么明目张胆--以及为什么它如此有问题。在有这么多其他事情发生的时候，我认识到这可能看起来不是优先事项，但它是疯狂的。法官实际上是在挑选案件--在这些案件中，他几乎不是一个公正的观察者，在被任命为法官之前，他曾是一名专利诉讼律师。正如我们多年来详细讨论的那样，法官与专利律师的关系越近，他们就越有可能支持专利流氓，而不会看到专利过于宽泛或专利流氓敲诈实际创新者的问题。
这篇论文还详细介绍了奥尔布赖特法官的做法如何帮助专利流氓(这可能解释了为什么提交给他的法院的这些专利案件中有85%来自流氓)。他推动案件进展得更快，这给公司带来了更大的压力，要求它们达成和解，以避免诉讼费用。而且，奥尔布赖特法官甚至比吉尔斯特拉普法官都要快得多，后者在马歇尔的法庭通常被称为“火箭法庭”，因为它处理专利流氓案件的速度很快。
那么，TC哈特兰最终开始向德克萨斯州东部的吉尔斯特拉普施压，要求将案件转移到适当的地点，这一决定又如何呢？
自从担任法官以来，奥尔布赖特法官同样坚定地拒绝将案件移交给西区。截至2020年7月7日，根据第1404(A)条，他已经决定了13项要求调离西区的动议；他拒绝了11项。事实上，在最近的一项命令中，奥尔布赖特法官有效地告诉苹果公司-在指派给奥尔布赖特法官的案件中，苹果已经被起诉至少十次，并定期寻求将这些案件转移到加州北区-停止提交转移动议。
他们确实注意到，联邦巡回法院最近命令奥尔布赖特移交他的一个涉及Adobe的案件，并给他目前未能定期移交案件蒙上了一些阴影。希望这意味着它开始更频繁地回击他。
再说一次，报纸上有更多的内容，联邦法院竟然允许这样做，这一切都太疯狂了。国会有很多事情要做，但它应该做些事情来解决奥尔布赖特法官出售的这一管辖权。
感谢您阅读这篇技术污垢帖子。这几天有这么多事情争相吸引大家的注意力，我们真的很感谢您抽出时间来参加我们的活动。我们每天都在努力工作，为我们的社区提供高质量的内容。
Techdle是为数不多的真正独立的媒体机构之一。我们背后没有一家大公司，我们在很大程度上依赖我们的社区来支持我们，在这个时代，广告商对赞助小型独立网站越来越不感兴趣-特别是像我们这样不愿在报告和分析方面出拳的网站。
虽然其他网站已经求助于付费墙、注册要求和越来越烦人的/侵入性广告，但我们一直保持Techdle对任何人开放和可用。但是为了继续这样做，我们需要你们的支持。我们为我们的读者提供了多种方式来支持我们，从直接捐款到特别订阅和酷商品-每一点都有帮助。谢谢。
note_en:
For years, you may recall that we would write about the insane nature of forum shopping for patent trolls, in which the trolls would flock to the federal courts in  East Texas. Going back nearly 15 years, we wrote about how East Texas courts became grand central for patent troll cases, leading to all sorts of sketchy behavior. There are a bunch of empty office buildings setup in small Texas cities (mainly Marshall and Tyler) just to "pretend" to have offices there. Companies engaged in many patent cases started to try to suck up to residents of those small cities, in case they might be on a jury. TiVo  literally bought a "Grand Champion Steer" just weeks before a jury was set to rule on a massive TiVo trolling case. Samsung threw so much money at the local "Stagecoach Days" event that it was renamed "Samsung Stagecoach Days," and built a  Samsung ice rink  right next to the courthouse in Marshall.
 For years, people pressured  Congress to fix this mess, but instead, the Supreme Court finally stepped in, with the TC Heartland ruling,  and said that the proper jurisdiction should be where defendants actually are incorporated. Of course, this seemed to have the reverse effect -- as companies no longer want to be in East Texas. Apple  shut down its stores there to avoid the jurisdiction.
 Of course, if you thought that the judges would go quietly, you'd be wrong. It's always felt like a few judges in East Texas  loved the reputation they'd built up as being super friendly to patent trolls. For a while it was Judge  T. John Ward. And when he left the bench (to become a patent lawyer, natch),  Judge Rodney Gilstrap stepped into the gap he left. He even tried to  ignore the Supreme Court's TC Heartland decision (though the Federal Circuit appeals court was  not impressed).
 However, as Patent Progress notes, there's  a new judge vying to be at the top of the patent troll's Christmas list, and he's in  West Texas. Judge Alan Albright, a former patent litigator, was appointed to the bench in 2018 -- and he literally  went on a tour to convince companies to bring patent cases in his court:
 U.S. District Judge Alan Albright and attorneys who predicted last year that Waco’s federal court would become a hotbed of patent and intellectual property litigation missed their prediction just a bit.
 With Albright traveling the country drumming up business and patent attorneys spreading the word that Waco’s new federal judge, a longtime patent litigator, will provide the expertise to create an efficient and welcoming environment in Waco, the response in the past year actually exceeded those predictions.
 Since Albright took office in September 2018, more than 250 patent cases have been filed in the federal Western District of Texas, which includes Waco. That total eclipses the number for the previous four years combined and has made the Western District among the busiest in the country for patent cases.
 It's hard to read that and not think... that's fucked up. A judge should not be "travelling the country drumming up business." There's a reason that forum shopping is a bad idea -- and this is even worse: it's  forum selling. As that article notes, more and more patent law firms are now setting up business in Waco, near Albright's court, and even the troll firms in East Texas are heading just a bit west:
 Waco law firm Haley & Olson announced in March it was combining forces with Mann, Tindel and Thompson, an East Texas firm that specializes in patent litigation, to capitalize on the new focus of the Waco federal court.
 That should be seen as a problem, but instead, it seems to be business as usual in the federal courts of Texas.
 Two law professors have now come up with a  new paper delving into this troubling trend, which kicks off with a pretty telling mocked up Craigslist ad, which they say "startlingly... accurately portrays what is happening right now in the U.S. District Court for theWestern District of Texas."
  The two professors -- J. Jonas Anderson and Paul Gugliuzza -- lay out the problems pretty starkly:
 One judge, appointed to the court less than twoyears ago, has been advertising his district—through presentations topatent lawyers, comments to the media, procedures in his courtroom, anddecisions in patent cases—as the place to file your patent infringementlawsuit. And he has succeeded. In 2018, the Western District receivedonly 90 patent cases—a mere 2.5% of patent suits nationwide. In 2020,the Western District is on track to receive more than 800—the most of anydistrict in the country. Importantly, these suits are overwhelmingly filedby so-called patent trolls—entities that don’t make any products orprovide services but instead exist solely to enforce patents.
 The centralization of patent cases before a single judge, acting entirelyon his own to seek out patent litigation, is facilitated by the WesternDistrict’s case filing system, which allows plaintiffs to choose not just thecourt but the specific judge who will hear their case. These dynamics—ajudge advertising for patent cases and plaintiffs shopping for thatjudge—undermine public confidence in the impartiality of the judiciary,make the court an uneven playing field for litigants, and facilitate thenuisance suits patent trolls favor.
 As they note, it seems like an easy thing to fix if Congress had the will:
 Two reforms would help solve thisproblem: first, district judges should—by law—be randomly assigned tocases and, second, venue in patent cases should be tied to geographicdivisions within a judicial district, not just the district as a whole.
 The article even details how  in the midst of the pandemic, both Judge Albright and Judge Gilstrap appear to be  competing to rush through as many patent cases as possible,  in person, so that more trolls will file in their courts:
 The COVID-19 pandemic has upended American life. Millions areunemployed. Many who still have jobs—grocery store workers, deliverydrivers, healthcare providers—risk their lives every day. Those of uswho are privileged have learned to work entirely from home; our biggestrisk is a dancing child or stray pet wandering into the background of aZoom meeting. COVID has disrupted the practice of law, too. Even theU.S. Supreme Court has yielded, holding oral arguments by telephoneand broadcasting them live for the first time. The U.S. Court of Appealsfor the Federal Circuit, which hears appeals of all patent casesnationwide, has likewise suspended in-person oral arguments untilfurther notice.
 But Judge Albright appears determined to forge ahead with patentlitigation as usual. Judges in other patent-heavy district courts are, too.Though some judges have compromised and held patent proceedings onZoom, Chief Judge Rodney Gilstrap of the Eastern District of Texas,who, until last year, heard more patent cases than any judge in thecountry, recently denied a defendant’s motion to delay an in-personjury trial slated to begin on August 3, quoting poet Robert Frost’s idiomthat “the best way out is always through.” What is going on with thesejudges?
 They are in the midst of a vigorous competition to attract patentcases to their courtrooms. As this article shows, Judge Albright iswinning. And this court competition is not good for the patent systemor the court system more broadly.
 As the article notes, this forum  selling is the unexpected flip side of historical forum shopping. And Judge Albright seems to have figured out how to sell his court like no judge before. One of the things he'll even do is let patent holders sue in his court in Waco, transfer the case to the more convenient court in Austin... but still have Judge Albright preside over the case. Here's a simple chart of how this has played out in practice:
  And again, nearly all of those are going to a single judge. If you don't see that as a problem, well, then you're probably a patent troll. Judge Albright isn't particularly shy about this either:
 Immediately upon his appointment as a district judge in 2018, JudgeAlbright went on a media blitz, letting everyone know that his courtwould welcome patent litigation. The Waco Tribune-Herald reportedthat Judge Albright “let it be known in no uncertain terms that he wouldlike his Waco courtroom to become a hub for IP cases.” He attendeddinners for patent litigators and patent owners to extoll the virtues oftrying patent cases in Waco. Judge Albright stated that he took theposition in Waco because he “‘thought it was the perfect place to try andestablish a serious venue for sophisticated patent litigation.’” Mosttellingly, he gave a presentation at the 2019 annual meeting of theAmerican Intellectual Property Law Association titled, “Why Youshould File Your Next Patent Case Across the Street from the ‘HeySugar,’” referring to a candy store near his Waco courthouse.
 You'd think a judge would recognize just how bad this looks, but I guess not.
 As the paper details, Judge Albright is the only judge in Waco, so if you file there, you're guaranteed to get him, and he's made it clear that he'll personally handle all the details of patent cases, farming out the grunt work on other cases to magistrate judges:
 Secure in the knowledge that patent cases filed in Waco will be heardby Judge Albright, patent plaintiffs are even more incentivized to file inWaco because of Judge Albright’s unique assignment orders to hismagistrate judge. Judge Albright assigns all cases to his magistrate tohandle all non-dispositive motions—except in patent, copyright, andcertain habeas corpus cases. Thus, patent plaintiffs know that JudgeAlbright will be personally involved in every aspect of the litigation andwon’t be distracted with other, non-patent cases on his docket. That levelof attention from a district judge during all stages of litigation isexceedingly rare. Delaware, for example, heavily uses magistrates inpatent cases. Even the Eastern District of Texas relies on magistratesto handle important motions and pre-trial hearings, including claimconstruction.
 For plaintiffs, choosing the Waco Division could not be simpler.Plaintiffs simply select “Waco” from a drop-down menu of divisions onthe Western District’s electronic case filing system and—voila!—thecase is automatically assigned to Judge Albright.
 The paper goes on to detail more and more and more crazy examples of just how blatant this all is -- and why it's so problematic. At a time when there is so much else going on, I recognize that this might not seem like a priority, but it's  insane. A judge is literally shopping for cases -- cases in which he's hardly an unbiased observer, having been a patent litigator before being appointed to the bench. As we've discussed at great length over the years, the closer judges are to patent lawyers, the  more likely they are to support patent trolling, and not see the problems with overly broad patents, or patent trolls shaking down actual innovators.
 The paper also details how Judge Albright's practices aid patent trolls (which may explain why 85% of these patent cases filed in his court are from trolls). He pushes the cases to move faster, which puts much more pressure on companies to settle to avoid the expense of litigation. And, Judge Albright is much faster than even Judge Gilstrap, whose court in Marshall was regularly called the "rocket docket" for its speed in handling patent troll cases.
 And what about that TC Heartland decision that finally started pressuring Gilstrap in East Texas to transfer cases to their proper venue?
 Since taking the bench, Judge Albright has likewise staunchlyrefused to transfer cases out of the Western District. As of July 7, 2020,he has decided thirteen motions seeking transfer away from the WesternDistrict under § 1404(a); he has denied eleven. In fact, in a recentorder, Judge Albright effectively told Apple—which has been sued atleast ten times in cases assigned to Judge Albright and regularly seeksto have those cases moved to the Northern District of California—to stopfiling transfer motions.
 They do note that the Federal Circuit recently  ordered Albright to transfer one of his cases, involving Adobe, and threw some shade at his current failure to regularly transfer cases. Hopefully that means it starts pushing back on him more often.
 Again, there's a lot more in the paper, and it's all crazy that this is allowed in a federal court. Congress has many things on its plate, but it should do something to fix this jurisdiction  selling by Judge Albright.
 Thank you for reading this Techdirt post. With so many things competing for everyone’s attention these days, we really appreciate you giving us your time. We work hard every day to put quality content out there for our community.
 Techdirt is one of the few remaining truly independent media outlets. We do not have a giant corporation behind us, and we rely heavily on our community to support us, in an age when advertisers are increasingly uninterested in sponsoring small, independent sites — especially a site like ours that is unwilling to pull punches in its reporting and analysis.
 While other websites have resorted to paywalls, registration requirements, and increasingly annoying/intrusive advertising, we have always kept Techdirt open and available to anyone. But in order to continue doing so,  we need your support. We offer a variety of ways for our readers to support us, from direct donations to special subscriptions and cool merchandise — and every little bit helps. Thank you.

作为周二广告周的一部分，谷歌推出了更多的自动化功能和活动类型。这些公告表明，谷歌多年来一直致力于利用机器学习服务于旗下各部门的宣传活动，同时也体现了谷歌为应对疫情所采取的举措。
谷歌广告副总裁兼总经理杰里·迪施勒(Jerry Dischler)周五在接受采访时表示：“我们现在有机会后退一步，弄清楚一些长期趋势是什么，我们要么创造新的努力，要么加快现有的努力，以试图迎合这些趋势，”谷歌广告副总裁兼总经理杰里·迪施勒(Jerry Dischler)周五在接受采访时表示。
今年5月，为了应对因居家订单而迅速改变的消费者行为，谷歌发布了Ring Retail Categories，这是一款面向快速增长的产品搜索的工具。在此基础上，谷歌广告用户界面中的一个新的洞察力页面将引入来自Ring Retail类别的数据，以及为该账户定制的Google Trends数据。
广告商将看到目前的趋势搜索，拍卖洞察力，以及根据他们的账户量身定做的兴趣预测。例如，一家户外用品零售商可能会认为“帐篷”是一个流行的术语，拍卖会上的竞争对手正在获得印象份额，而“滑雪”预计将在11月成为潮流。它还集成了建议(如下所示)。
Insight将在未来几个月以测试版推出，第一次在今年第四季度进入美国和英国。谷歌表示，它将增加受众和预测数据。Dischler说：“我们希望这只是一个基础，我们将在此基础上建立基础，以便直接在谷歌广告中提供更好的商业洞察力。”
Performance Max是谷歌最新的自动化活动类型。“我们致力于自动化已经有很长一段时间了，对于很多广告商来说，Covid真的感觉是一个转折点，因为当你对系统造成如此大的冲击，而且你是手动操作的时候，负载是如此之高的…。你必须专注于其他事情，比如经营你的企业。“迪施勒说。
与其他自动化选项(通用应用活动、本地活动、智能活动、智能购物活动)不同的是，最高性能运行在所有库存中。Performance Max活动广告将有资格在Display Network、YouTube、Gmail、Discover、Search等网站上投放，旨在补充标准的搜索活动。搜索和搜索合作伙伴的广告不是基于关键字的，而是通过动态搜索广告提供的。
与其他自动广告类型一样，广告商将提供文案、图像和/或视频资产，谷歌的机器学习系统将自动为这些渠道的响应性广告提供服务，并根据广告商设定的目标使用自动智能竞价进行竞价。
我们的想法是建立一个活动，覆盖谷歌拥有和运营的所有资产，而不是为特定的渠道创建专门的广告。新的洞察力页面还将包括有关Performance Max活动的详细信息，包括建议，如下例所示。
广告商将能够提供一些手动输入，通过指定最有可能转换的受众并使用转换值规则来指示哪些转换对业务最有价值，从而帮助告知机器学习算法。当您的帐户中启用了转换时，您可以在测量下的转换部分找到值规则。您可以根据位置、设备和受众调整值。然后，这些规则将应用于谷歌的聪明竞标。
Performance Max活动仍处于早期测试阶段。明年将邀请更多的广告商参加测试版。
谷歌将在未来几周向所有广告商发布视频行动活动。这些以表现为重点的活动在YouTube和谷歌视频合作伙伴上进行。
该公司还在测试电视屏幕的直接响应视频广告格式。谷歌在5月份表示，每月有超过1亿用户在电视屏幕上观看YouTube和YouTube电视。Dischler说：“我们有做得非常好的品牌广告商，但直接反应一直难以捉摸。”他说，他们看到了早期直接反应测试的进展。电视屏幕上还会显示视频活动清单。
更正：本文已更新，反映了最高绩效活动可以通过动态搜索广告在搜索库存上运行。

note_en:
Google introduced more automation features and campaign types as part of Advertising Week Tuesday. The  announcements speak to what has been a years-long effort to use machine learning to serve campaigns across Google’s properties as well as to the moves it’s made in response to the pandemic.
 “We’ve now had a chance to take a step back and figure out what some of the long-term trends are, and we either create a new effort or accelerate existing efforts in order to try to play into those trends,” Jerry Dischler, vice president and GM of Ads at Google, said in an interview Friday.
  In May, in response to quickly shifting consumer behaviors as a result of stay-at-home orders, Google  released Rising Retail Categories, a tool that surfaces fast-growing product searches. Building on that, a new Insights page in the Google Ads UI will bring in data from Rising Retail Categories as well as Google Trends data that is customized to the account.
 Advertisers will see currently trending searches, auction insights as well as interest predictions tailored to their account. For example, an outdoor gear retailer might see that “tents” is a trending term, that competitors in the auction are gaining impression share and that “skiing” is predicted to trend in November. It also integrates with recommendations (as shown below).
  Insights will be rolling out in beta in the coming months, first coming to the U.S. and UK in the fourth-quarter of this year. Google says it will be adding audience and forecasting data. “We’re hoping that this is just going to be a foundation that we’re going to build on top of in order to offer better business insights directly within Google Ads,” said Dischler.
   Performance Max is Google’s newest automated campaign type. “We’ve been working on automation for a long time, and COVID really felt like a turning point for a lot of advertisers, because when you have a big shock to the system like this and you’re operating things manually, the load is so high … You’ve got to be focusing on other things like running your business,” said Dischler.
 Unlike other automated options — Universal App campaigns, Local campaigns, Smart campaigns, Smart Shopping campaigns — Performance Max run across all inventory. Performance Max campaign ads will be eligible to serve on the Display Network, YouTube, Gmail, Discover, Search etc. and are meant to complement standard Search campaigns. Search and search partners ads aren’t keyword-based and are served via dynamic search ads.
 Like the other automated campaign types, advertisers will supply copy and image and/or video assets, and Google’s machine learning systems will automatically serve the responsive ads across those channels and bid using automated Smart Bidding based on the goal the advertiser sets.
 The idea is to set up one campaign to reach across all Google owned and operated properties rather than create specialized ads for specific channels. The new Insights page will also include details on Performance Max campaigns, including recommendations, as shown in the example below.
  Advertisers will be able to provide some manual inputs to help inform the machine learning algorithms by specifying audiences that are most likely to convert and using  conversion value rules to indicate which conversions are most valuable to the business. You’ll find value rules in the Conversions section under Measurement when it’s enabled in your account. You can adjust values based on location, device and audience. The rules are then applied to Google’s smart bidding.
 Performance Max campaigns remain in early testing. More advertisers will be invited to the beta next year.
  Google is releasing Video action campaigns to all advertisers in the coming weeks. The performance-focused campaigns run on YouTube and Google video partners.
 The company is also testing direct-response video ad formats for TV screens. Google said in May  that over 100 million users are watching YouTube and YouTube TV on their television screens monthly. “We’ve got brand advertisers that are doing very well, but direct response has been elusive,” said Dischler. He said they are seeing progress with early direct-response tests. Video action campaigns also show on television screen inventory.
 Correction:  This article has been updated to reflect that Performance Max campaigns can run on Search inventory via dynamic search ads.
       Ginny Marvin is Third Door Media’s Editor-in-Chief, running the day to day editorial operations across all publications and overseeing paid media coverage. Ginny Marvin writes about paid digital advertising and analytics news and trends for Search Engine Land, Marketing Land and MarTech Today. With more than 15 years of marketing experience, Ginny has held both in-house and agency management positions. She can be found on Twitter as @ginnymarvin.

门户电视是一种可以在家里与电视一起工作的媒体设备，如今它提供了一系列流媒体服务，包括Amazon Prime Video、Showtime和Sling TV。但是，到目前为止，最重要的流媒体服务之一-Netflix-一直不见踪影。Facebook表示，现在将在所有销售Portal的国家将Netflix引入Portal TV。
该公司还将推出一款新的遥控器，其中将包括启动流媒体应用程序的一键式按钮，如Prime Video、Netflix和自己的Facebook Watch。从今天开始，这款产品将与新的Portal电视设备一起发货，并将在未来几周内在商店销售。
在Portal TV与Netflix整合的消息传出之前不久，亚马逊(Amazon)9月份宣布，将在自己的亚马逊Echo Show智能显示屏上支持这项广受欢迎的流媒体服务。Echo Show系列最初亮相三年后。谷歌最近也将Netflix加入了其智能显示器阵容，并于今年早些时候在其Nest Hub和Hub Max上提供了支持。
这些更新的时机暗示，与Netflix就智能显示器集成的谈判一直在全行业进行，因为Netflix正在努力使其服务跨各种平台可用。
Netflix昨晚深夜开始通过更新向Portal TV部门推出，所以所有Portal TV用户现在都应该可以使用这款应用了。
Facebook表示，它还将Zoom引入其门户设备，用于视频通话。今年8月，Zoom确认了支持智能显示器的计划，包括Echo Show和Google Nest Hub Max，以及Facebook的Portal。
Facebook表示，有了门户支持，Zoom用户将能够在屏幕上主持最多25人的视频通话，同时利用该设备的高保真声音和AI驱动的智能摄像头进行免提通话。该支持将添加到设备销售的所有地区的Portal Mini、Portal和Portal+。
正如之前在Facebook Connect上宣布的那样，该公司还在扩大其Story Time库中的互动故事集，提供更多样化的内容。新故事包括谢谢你，OMU！，一本关于归属感的儿童书和奶奶的钱包。此外，AR功能正在被添加到Seuss博士的故事中，包括Hop on Pop，以及Brown Mr Can Moo！你能吗？，拿出足书，我的口袋里有一只手表！这些东西将在今年秋天的某个时候推出。
门户网站的增强现实功能也在升级。用户将能够使用他们的声音和“嘿门户”命令在照相亭控制AR效果，发送主题卡片，并拍摄带有AR效果的照片和视频发送给朋友和家人。
“嘿，门户网站”现在除了英语外，还将支持美国西班牙语，预计未来还会有更多的语言。但是，门户网站上的屏幕文本可以用法语、西班牙语、意大利语和英语显示。
note_en:
Portal TV, the media device that works with your TV at home, today offers access to a range of streaming services, including Amazon Prime Video, Showtime and Sling TV. But, until now, one of the most important streaming services — Netflix — had been missing. Facebook says Netflix will now be introduced to Portal TV in all countries where Portal is sold.
 It will also introduce a new remote that will include one-touch buttons for launching streaming apps, like Prime Video, Netflix and its own Facebook Watch. This will ship with new Portal TV devices starting today and will be sold in stores in the coming weeks.
  The news of Portal TV’s Netflix integration follows shortly after  Amazon’s September announcement that it would support the popular streaming service on its own Amazon Echo Show smart displays, three years after the Echo Show line’s original debut. Google, too,  recently added Netflix to its smart display lineup, with support arriving on its Nest Hub and Hub Max earlier this year.
  The timing of these updates hint that negotiations with Netflix over smart display integrations had been taking place industry-wide, as Netflix worked to make to make its service available across the various platforms.
 Netflix began rolling out to Portal TV units via an update late last night, so all Portal TV users should have the app available now.
  Facebook is also bringing  Zoom   to its Portal devices for video calling, it says. In August, Zoom had  confirmed its plans to support smart displays, including Echo Show and Google Nest Hub Max, as well as Portal from Facebook.
 With Portal support, Zoom users will be able to host a video call with up to 25 people on the screen, while leveraging the device’s high-fidelity sound and its AI-powered Smart Camera for hands-free calling, says Facebook. The support will be added to Portal Mini, Portal and Portal+ in all regions where the device is sold.
  As previously announced at Facebook Connect, the company is also expanding with more diverse content its collection of interactive stories in its Story Time library. New stories include  Thank You, Omu!,  A Kids Book About Belonging and  Grandma’s Purse. Plus, AR features are being added to Dr. Seuss stories, including  Hop on Pop,  Mr. Brown Can Moo! Can You?,  The Foot Book and  There’s a Wocket in my Pocket! These will roll out sometime this fall.
 Portal’s AR features are getting an update, too. Users will be able to control AR effects in Photo Booth using their voice and the “Hey Portal” command to send themed cards and take photos and video with AR effects to send to friends and family.
 “Hey Portal” will also now work in U.S. Spanish, in addition to English, with more languages expected to come in the future. On-screen text on Portal can be displayed in French, Spanish, Italian and English, however.

跳到主要内容一名乌干达环保活动家在一场高调的竞选活动中被Twitter停职-他认为这一停职与他在该国政府和与砍伐森林有关的行业中的反对者有关。推特不愿透露账户被冻结的原因，但环保组织担心，这是强大的利益相关者利用推特的温和系统让气候活动家保持沉默的更广泛趋势的一部分。
停职发生在9月12日晚上，当时22岁的Nyombi Morris刚刚结束了一次关于布戈马森林保护的电视露面。面试后的第二天早上，他醒来时发现自己的账户被冻结了，没有任何解释。他说，在他的账户被暂停的几周内，他至少联系了Twitter的帮助中心五次，但找不到是什么触发了冻结，并开始怀疑暂停可能与他的倡导有关。另一位与森林砍伐作斗争的乌干达活动家莉亚·纳穆格瓦(Leah Namugerwa)也在9月份被冻结了她的账户。
“我非常非常失望，”莫里斯告诉The Verge。“我怀疑的是，因为我们的政府试图让我们在布戈马森林的问题上保持沉默，他们利用一些人暂停了我们的账户。”在The Verge就暂停一事联系Twitter后，莫里斯于9月30日重新访问了他的原始账户。莫里斯和The Verge一开始都没有被告知账户被暂停的原因。
推特表示，至少有两个被暂停的环保活动人士账号被垃圾邮件过滤器捕获，此后已被恢复。这家社交媒体巨头没有提供更多信息，说明为什么这些账户可能被标记为垃圾账户，其他账户发生了什么，或者总共有多少账户被暂停。
这一暂停引起了世界各地的关注。以青少年活动家格里塔·通伯格(Greta Thunberg)在瑞典举行的气候罢工为开端的全球运动--未来周五(Friays For Future)于9月28日发布了一条推文，呼吁人们关注今年在世界各地被暂停的众多账号。“这是不可接受的。拥有一个谈论气候危机的平台至关重要，特别是对(受气候变化影响最大的人和地区)而言，“该组织称。
越来越多的气候活动家的账户被暂停。这是不可接受的。拥有一个谈论气候危机的平台是至关重要的，特别是对MAPA来说。回复您知道已暂停的任何帐户(城市/国家)。我们会尽力而为的！
-未来的星期五(@Fridays4Future)2020年9月28日。
莫里斯说，账户冻结使他与其他活动人士隔绝，并扼杀了他说话的能力。“我们没有得到我们媒体的支持。能支持我们的只有社交媒体，但即使在社交媒体上我们也沉默了--我们还能去哪里呢？“。莫里斯说。
根据乌干达国家林业局(NFA)的数据，乌干达的森林砍伐速度令人震惊，仅在过去25年里，那里就有63%的森林被砍伐。有争议的布戈马森林是国际自然保护联盟(International Union For Protection Of Nature)濒危物种红色名录上9种哺乳动物的家园，其中包括大约600只濒危黑猩猩。环保人士说，保护森林对人们来说也很重要，因为当地人依赖于它的资源，它是吸收和储存导致全球变暖的二氧化碳的重要碳汇。
2016年，乌干达的布约罗-基塔拉王国将布戈马森林的部分区域租给了糖业制造商Hoima Sugar。(乌干达在英国殖民之前是由王国组成的，他们的统治者今天得到了国家的承认，尽管他们没有被授予主权。)。管理Bugoma的国家林业局将Bunyoro-Kitara国王和Hoima Sugar告上了法庭，声称该公司没有合法权利清理森林，为糖料种植园让路。乌干达高等法院去年在一项裁决中做出了不利于NFA的裁决，国家环境管理局(National Environment Management Authority)今年批准了将土地用于糖料种植园。
目前还不清楚是什么触发了莫里斯的停职，也没有确凿的证据表明这与他的政治对手有关-但这对他的生活和工作的影响是不可否认的。此前，由于Twitter的影响力，他比任何其他社交媒体平台都更依赖Twitter。他通过推特认识了世界各地的积极分子，他觉得自己听到了有影响力的人的耳朵。格里塔·通伯格跟着他，他自豪地提到。他说，Twitter也是他可以了解更多关于世界其他地区的环境和问题的地方。
他的账号关闭后，他开始从他创办的一个倡导团体的另一个账号发推文。但它的追随者要少得多，莫里斯与一些不知道他的新账户的朋友失去了联系。他在社交媒体上的存在似乎已经沦为耳语。他可以看到每个人都在说话，但他的声音却不一样。
“我不再教书了。现在我在那里(在Twitter上)学习，但我不能教书。这是最大的挑战，“莫里斯在他的账户仍被冻结时告诉The Verge。
我只是不知道该如何感谢你，但是感谢那些尽了最大努力的人看到我发布的账号正式回来了，我重申我的账号又回来了，现在我可以发推特了。请再次欢迎我。Pic.twitter.com/ejcifB13dH。
-Nyombi Morris(@NolanQ13)2020年10月1日。
这一经历与最初将他带到Twitter的一切背道而驰。他说：“社交媒体不会区分富人和穷人，它们让每个人都能参与其中--这就是我如此喜欢社交媒体的原因。”
在冠状病毒引发的封锁期间，社交媒体一直是环保活动人士更大的生命线，特别是在它伴随着政治动荡的时候。乌干达总统约韦里·穆塞韦尼(Yoweri Museveni)自1986年以来一直在掌权，他将参加明年的选举。疫情遏制了竞选集会和抗议活动，因为该国仍然不允许举行大规模集会，该国采取了一些非洲最严格的措施来遏制新冠肺炎的传播。这导致人们在网上变得更加直言不讳，政府随后试图更多地打击博客和社交媒体。乌干达自2018年以来一直对使用社交媒体网站征税，这被批评为对言论自由的攻击。
当大流行第一次开始展开时，包括Thunberg在内的示威者将他们的行动转移到了网上，并敦促他们的追随者遵守关于社会距离的公共卫生指导方针。9月25日，一些环保积极分子重新走上街头，发起了一场全球气候罢工。
在乌干达，抗议者因加入罢工而被捕-包括未来领导人利亚·纳穆格瓦(Leah Namugerwa)的周五。“作为一名气候活动家，这正在成为一种犯罪。如果我们走上街头，他们准备逮捕我们，“莫里斯说。这就是为什么他和其他人选择发布自己家中挂着抗议标语的照片和视频，而不是那天走上街头。莫里斯认为，那些被逮捕的人最终被释放，部分原因是其他人在社交媒体上对此大吵大闹。
我们的#气候冲刺被乌干达警方切断了。我们的标语牌都被没收了，大多数积极分子都被带到了警察那里。被捕的人包括@NamugerwaLeah@NakabuyeHildaF@SadrachNirere@LauraMuwanguzi@Matovu_bob&amp；其他#气候正义#GlobalClimate Strike pic.twitter.com/WVMGcOoMmx。
-未来乌干达的星期五(@Friday s4FutureU)2020年9月25日。
Twitter暂停对在反对工业和政府更危险的地方工作的积极分子产生了过大的影响。在世界许多地方，保护你生活的环境是一件致命的事情：2019年有212名环保活动人士被杀，这是监督组织全球见证(Global Witness)有记录以来最高的。
今年遇难者之一是菲律宾的兰迪·埃查尼斯(Randy Echanis)，他是环境、工人和农民的长期倡导者，8月份被发现在自己的公寓里被刺死。不久之后，菲律宾的活动人士在Twitter上呼吁恢复埃查尼斯担任主席的左翼政党阿纳克帕维斯(Anakpawis)的账户。他们说，在埃查尼斯的葬礼游行之后，该账户被暂停；现在它又重新上线了。埃查尼斯遇害前一个月，菲律宾总统罗德里戈·杜特尔特(Rodrigo Duterte)签署了一项反恐怖主义法案，人权监督机构表示，该法案将简单批评他的政府的人定为犯罪。他的政府监督了数千名菲律宾人的法外处决。根据全球见证组织(Global Witness)的数据，菲律宾一直是土地和环境捍卫者遇害的前三名国家之一。
我们谦虚地呼吁@Twitter@TwitterSupport重新激活@AnakpawisPL的账户，该账户自遇害的环保卫士和该组织主席兰迪·埃查尼斯(Randy Echanis)的埋葬游行以来一直处于暂停状态。你能做同样的事@YACAPhilippia@Fridays4Future@AghamDiliman pic.twitter.com/JtJPUVSLfm
-Pamalakaya Pilipinas(@para_pil)2020年9月28日。
推特并不是唯一一家社交媒体巨头，环保活动人士呼吁扼杀他们。Facebook上月面临审查，此前该公司暂停了200多名与抗议加拿大有争议的Coastal GasLink管道建设活动有关的人的账户。美国绿色和平组织首席项目官林赛·艾伦(Lindsey Allen)在9月份告诉The Verge，“Facebook可以随意压制大部分气候运动，而不会在任何时间内做出解释。”“这让人感到不安。”
莫里斯说，他很高兴自己又回到了推特上。Namugerwa的账户在9月28日未来的星期五推特上发布了关于停职的消息后不久就恢复了。“我简直不敢相信，我以为我失去了一切，”莫里斯说。他希望提高被冻结在账户之外的其他环保活动人士的意识，这对那些来自政治影响力较小的地方的人来说尤其重要，这些地方仍然首当其冲地承受着气候变化的危害。他们将失去的是平台的突发奇想。
note_en:
Skip to main content                                                                                                                A Ugandan environmental activist was suspended from Twitter in the midst of a high-profile campaign — a suspension he believes is connected to his opponents in the country’s government and industries linked to deforestation. Twitter won’t say what caused the account to be frozen, but environmental groups worry it’s part of a broader trend of powerful stakeholders exploiting Twitter’s moderation system to silence climate activists.
 The suspension happened on the night of September 12th, after 22-year-old Nyombi Morris had just finished a television appearance about the preservation of the Bugoma Forest. The morning after the interview, he woke up to find  his account was frozen without explanation. He says he contacted Twitter’s Help Center at least five times during the weeks his account was suspended but couldn’t figure out what had triggered the freeze, and began to suspect the suspension could be connected to his advocacy. Another Ugandan activist with Fridays for Future who fights deforestation, Leah Namugerwa, had  her account frozen in September, too.
  “I was very, very disappointed,” Morris told  The Verge. “What I suspected is that because our government was trying to silence us about this Bugoma Forest, they used some people to suspend our accounts.” Morris regained access to his original account on September 30th, after  The Verge contacted Twitter regarding the suspensions. Neither Morris nor  The Verge was told why the account was suspended in the first place.
 Twitter says that at least two of the suspended accounts belonging to environmental activists were caught in a spam filter and have since been reinstated. The social media giant didn’t give any more information on why those accounts might have been flagged as spam, what happened to other accounts, or how many in all have been suspended.
 The suspension has drawn concern from around the world. Fridays for Future, the global movement that started with teen activist Greta Thunberg’s climate strikes in Sweden, posted a  tweet on September 28th to call attention to numerous accounts suspended around the world this year. “This is unacceptable. Having a platform to talk about the climate crisis is crucial, especially for [people and areas most affected by climate change],” the group said.
 More and more climate activists' accounts are being suspended. This is unacceptable. Having a platform to talk about the climate crisis is crucial, especially for MAPA.  Reply with any accounts that you know have been suspended (with city/country). We’ll do our best to help!
— Fridays For Future (@Fridays4future)  September 28, 2020
 Morris says the account freeze isolated him from other activists and stifled his ability to speak out. “We are not supported by our media. It is only social media which can support us, but if even on social media we are silenced — where will we go?” says Morris.
 Deforestation has taken place at an alarming rate in Uganda, where 63 percent of forests have been logged in the past 25 years alone, according to the country’s National Forestry Authority (NFA). The contested Bugoma Forest is home to nine mammal species on the International Union for Conservation of Nature Red List of Threatened Species, including about 600 endangered chimpanzees. Protecting the forest is also important for people, conservationists say, because locals depend on its resources, and it functions as an important carbon sink that soaks up and stores planet-heating carbon dioxide.
 Parts of Bugoma Forest were leased to the sugar manufacturer Hoima Sugar by the  Bunyoro-Kitara Kingdom in Uganda in 2016. (Uganda was made up of kingdoms prior to British colonization, whose rulers are recognized by the country today even though they’re not given sovereign power.) The National Forestry Authority, which manages Bugoma, took the Bunyoro-Kitara king and Hoima Sugar to court, claiming that the company didn’t have the legal right to clear the forest to make way for sugar plantations. The Uganda High Court  ruled against the NFA in a decision last year, and the National Environment Management Authority  approved the use of land for sugar plantations this year.
  It’s still unclear what triggered Morris’ suspension, and there’s no hard evidence linking it to his political opponents — but its impact on his life and work is undeniable. He previously relied on Twitter more than any other social media platform because of its reach. He’d met activists around the world through Twitter, and he felt that he had the ear of influential people. Greta Thunberg followed him, he proudly mentioned. Twitter is also where he could learn more about the environment and issues in other parts of the world, he says.
 With his account down, he began tweeting from  another account for an advocacy group that he started. But it has far fewer followers, and Morris lost touch with some of his friends who didn’t know about his new account. It was as if his social media presence had been reduced to a whisper. He could see everyone talking, but he wasn’t being heard in the same way.
 “I’m no longer teaching. Now I’m there [on Twitter] to learn, but I cannot teach. That’s the biggest challenge,” Morris told  The Verge while his account was still frozen.
 I just don't know how to thank you, but thanks to whoever tried his/her level best to see that my account released   Am officially back, I repeat my account is back, am now able to tweet.. please welcome me once again.  pic.twitter.com/ejcifB13dH
— Nyombi Morris (@NolanQ13)  October 1, 2020
 The experience went against everything that brought him to Twitter in the first place. “Social media doesn’t separate the rich and the poor, they allow everyone — that’s why I like it so much,” he says.
 Social media has been an even bigger lifeline for environmental activists during coronavirus-induced lockdowns, especially when it’s paired with political turbulence. Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, who has been in power since 1986, is up for election next year. The pandemic curbed campaign rallies and protests since mass gatherings still aren’t allowed in the country, which has had some of the  strictest measures in place in Africa to curb the spread of COVID-19. That’s led to people becoming  more outspoken online, and the government subsequently attempting to crack down more on blogs and social media. Uganda has imposed  a tax on using social media sites since 2018, which has been criticized as an attack on free speech.
 When the pandemic first began to unfold, demonstrators, including Thunberg,  moved their actions online and urged their followers to follow public health guidelines on social distancing. Some environmental activists returned to the streets on September 25th for a Global Climate Strike.
 In Uganda,  protesters were arrested for joining the strike — including Fridays for Future leader Leah Namugerwa. “Being a climate activist, it is becoming a crime. They are ready to arrest us if we go to the streets,” Morris says. That’s why he and others opted to post photos and videos of themselves with protest signs from their homes rather than hit the streets that day. Those who were arrested were eventually released, in part because others made a ruckus about it on social media, Morris believes.
 Our  #ClimateStrike was cut short by Uganda police. Our placards were all grabbed, confiscated & most activists taken to police. Among the arrested one include  @NamugerwaLeah  @NakabuyeHildaF  @SadrachNirere  @LauraMuwanguzi  @matovu_bob & others #ClimateJustice  #GlobalClimateStrike  pic.twitter.com/WVMGcOoMmx
— Fridays For Future Uganda (@Fridays4FutureU)  September 25, 2020
 Twitter suspensions have an outsized impact on activists working in places where it’s more dangerous to oppose industry and the government. Defending the environment where you live is a deadly business in many places around the world: 212 environmental activists were killed in 2019, the highest ever recorded by  watchdog group Global Witness.
 One of those  killed this year was Randy Echanis, a longtime advocate for the environment, workers, and peasants, in the Philippines who was found stabbed to death in his apartment in August. Soon after, activists in the Philippines appealed to Twitter to reinstate the account of the left-wing political party, Anakpawis, that Echanis chaired. They said the account was suspended after the burial march for Echanis; it’s now back online. The month before Echanis was killed, Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte signed an Anti-Terrorism Act into law that human rights  watchdogs say criminalizes people for simply criticizing his government, which has overseen the  extrajudicial killings of thousands of Filipinos. The Philippines consistently ranks among the top three countries where land and environmental defenders are killed, according to Global Witness.
 We are humbly appealing to  @Twitter  @TwitterSupport to reactivate the account of  @AnakpawisPL, which has been suspended since the burial march of slain environmental defender and the group’s Chair Randy Echanis.   Can you do the same  @YACAPhilippines  @Fridays4future  @AghamDiliman  pic.twitter.com/JtJPUVSLfm
— Pamalakaya Pilipinas (@pama_pil)  September 28, 2020
 Twitter isn’t the only social media giant environmental activists have called out for stifling them. Facebook faced scrutiny last month after it suspended the accounts of more than 200 people connected to an event protesting the construction of the contested Coastal GasLink pipeline in Canada. “Facebook can silence much of the climate movement at their discretion, without explanation for any duration,” Lindsey Allen, chief program officer at Greenpeace USA,  told  The Verge in September. “That’s unnerving.”
 Morris says he’s happy that he’s back on Twitter. Namugerwa’s account was restored shortly after Fridays for Future tweeted about the suspensions on September 28th. “I couldn’t believe it, I thought that I lost everything,” Morris says. He wants to raise awareness for other environmental activists frozen out of their accounts, which is especially important for those from places with less political clout that nonetheless bear the brunt of harms from climate change. What they’ll lose is up to the whims of the platform.
title: 随着推特恢复两名乌干达活动人士的账户，环保组织担心推特的温和系统正被利用来压制活动人士
title_en: As Twitter reinstates accounts of two Ugandan activists, environmental groups worry that Twitter's moderation system is being exploited to silence activists

Facebook周一在一篇博客文章中表示，随着冠状病毒大流行在全球范围内加剧心理健康问题，Facebook将推出一个新的情感健康资源中心，提供专家指南和信息，将社交媒体平台及其拥有的其他应用程序的用户与服务和支持联系起来。
情绪健康将是Facebook应用程序上的一个集中资源中心，提供来自世界各地专家的提示和信息，包括NAMI、儿童帮助电话和It‘s OK to Talk。该中心将在全球范围内使用，并提供来自精神卫生官员的有关您所在地区的信息。
为我们的编辑订阅CNET Now时事通讯，精选当天最重要的新闻。
你也可以在Facebook旗下的其他应用程序中找到与心理健康相关的功能。世界卫生组织数字压力管理指南提供了减少压力的提示，现在可以在WhatsApp上的世卫组织健康警报聊天机器人上找到。在Messenger上，你会发现与世卫组织共同设计的贴纸，通过危机文本专线在应用程序上促进关于精神健康的对话，以及自杀和自我伤害预防。在Instagram上，你会发现新的针对青少年和其他群体的心理健康指南。
尊重他人，保持文明礼貌，不离题。我们删除违反我们政策的评论，我们鼓励您阅读这些评论。我们可以随时关闭讨论线程。
note_en:
As the   coronavirus pandemic exacerbates mental health problems worldwide,  Facebook is launching a new  emotional health resource center with expert guides and information, to connect users of the social media platform and the other apps it owns with services and support, the company said in a blog post on Monday.
 Emotional Health will be a centralized resource center on the Facebook app with tips and information from experts worldwide, including  NAMI,  Kids Help Phone and  It's OK To Talk. The hub will be available globally, with information about your area from mental health officials.
  Subscribe to the CNET Now newsletter for our editors' picks of the most important stories of the day.
 You'll find mental health-related features across the other apps owned by Facebook as well. The World Health Organization Digital Stress Management Guide, which provides tips to reduce stress, is now available on the WHO Health Alert chatbot on  WhatsApp. On Messenger, you'll find stickers designed with WHO to facilitate conversations about mental health, as well as suicide and self-harm prevention on the app through  Crisis Text Line. And on  Instagram, you'll find new mental well-being guides for teens and other groups.

note:
10月是国际增强和另类沟通(AAC)认知月，谷歌正在努力让Assistant在Tobii Dynvox设备上可用，并更新Action Block以提供更熟悉的体验。
谷歌今天宣布了与Tobii Dynvox的合作伙伴关系，Tobii Dynvox是一家专门生产辅助设备和移动应用的公司。该公司提供的平板电脑具有“预先配置的一套瓷砖，供用户就日常事务进行交流”，可以通过触摸、眼睛凝视或扫描进行控制。
助手现在可以是设备上一个磁贴，既可以控制智能家居设备，也可以向谷歌提问。
我们还与Tobii密切合作，使这种体验易于设置。首先，创建一个Google账户，并在Android或iOS上的Google Home应用程序中设置智能扬声器或智能显示器。在提供对Snap Core First应用程序的访问后，你可以通过在编辑模式下选择一个按钮，点击“添加操作”，然后点击“发送谷歌助手命令”来配置磁贴。
与此同时，谷歌正在使用Tobii Dynvox的图片交流符号库更新Action Block，该符号库可以用作助手主屏幕按钮的封面图像。总共有数以万计的符号，人们可能已经熟悉了。
同时，动作块现在可以直接分配给交换机。因此，“轻触物理按钮可以直接触发Google Assistant操作。”
区块现在可以设置为说：“是”、“不”、“对不起，我有话要说”或其他常见的短语。
你正在阅读朝九晚五的谷歌新闻，这些专家日复一日地发布关于谷歌及其周边生态系统的新闻。请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新新闻，并关注Twitter、Facebook和LinkedIn上的朝九晚五谷歌，随时了解最新消息。不知道从何说起？查看我们的独家故事、评论、操作指南，并订阅我们的YouTube频道
note_en:
October is International Augmentative and Alternative Communication (AAC) Awareness Month, and Google is working to make Assistant available on Tobii Dynavox devices, as well as updating  Action Blocks to provide a more familiar experience.
  Google today announced an Assistant partnership with Tobii Dynavox, a  maker of dedicated assistive devices and mobile apps. The company offers tablets that feature a “preconfigured set of tiles for users to communicate about everyday things,” and can be controlled by touch, eye gaze, or scanning.
 Assistant can now be a tile on that device to both control smart home gadgets and ask Google questions.
 We’ve also worked closely with Tobii to make this experience easy to set up. First, create a Google account and set up a smart speaker or smart display in the Google Home app on Android or iOS. After providing access to the Snap Core First app, you can then configure tiles by selecting a button in edit mode, tapping on “Add action” and then tapping on “Send Google Assistant command.”
 Meanwhile, Google is updating  Action Blocks with a library of Picture Communication Symbols from Tobii Dynavox that can be used as the cover image for Assistant homescreen buttons. There are tens of thousands of symbols in total that people might already be familiar with.
 Meanwhile, Action Blocks can now be assigned directly to switches. As such, “tapping a physical button can directly trigger a Google Assistant action.”
 Blocks can now be set up to say: “Yes,” “No,” “Excuse me, I have something to say,” or other common phrases.
  You’re reading 9to5Google — experts who break news about Google and its surrounding ecosystem, day after day. Be sure to check out  our homepage for all the latest news, and follow 9to5Google on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  exclusive stories,  reviews,  how-tos, and  subscribe to our YouTube channel
title: 谷歌通过与Tobii Dynvox的合作将Assistant与专用辅助设备集成在一起，并使Action Block更容易访问
title_en: Google integrates Assistant with dedicated assistive devices through a partnership with Tobii Dynavox and makes Action Blocks more accessible

note:
该公司开发自己的故事产品已经有一段时间了。2018年，它推出了AMP Stories，这是基于为谷歌加速移动页面项目开发的技术。这些故事已经整合到移动谷歌搜索中，旨在为谷歌提供自己的故事选择，以取代Snapchat、Instagram和Facebook等其他应用程序提供的故事。但是，在谷歌的案例中，故事集中在出版商的内容上。
今天，谷歌将这种视觉内容称为“网络故事”，而不是AMP故事，并将这种体验整合到美国、印度和巴西的谷歌搜索应用程序中。
在这里，用户将看到Discover选项卡顶部的Web故事行，在那里他们可以点击进入全屏故事体验。像在其他应用程序中找到的故事一样，你可以点击前进到Google Web故事的下一页，或者滑动移动到旋转木马中的另一个故事。
谷歌指出，出版商负责创作他们的故事，并控制故事的货币化、托管和分享，以及为故事添加链接。要创建这些故事，出版商可以使用拖放工具，比如WordPress的Web Story编辑器、MakeStories或NewsroomAI。技术用户可以选择自己编写Web故事。
该公司表示，这种格式的早期采用者一直在他们的主页、社交频道、时事通讯等网站上使用网络故事，并将它们放在谷歌搜索中。
谷歌一直在与多家出版商合作，帮助他们为搜索和现在的原生移动应用程序创建网络故事。合作伙伴包括“福布斯”、“副刊”、“Refinery29”、“今日美国”、“孤独星球”、“Now This”、“Thrillist”、“PopSugar”、“渡渡鸟”、“Bustle”、“Input”、“尼龙”、“好莱坞报道”、Blativity、PC Gamer、“高尔夫周刊”等。谷歌说，出版商和谷歌在新产品上进行了合作，并帮助构建了新产品的功能。
到目前为止，已有2000多个网站发布了被谷歌编入索引的报道。
note_en:
The company has been developing its own Stories product for some time. In 2018, it  introduced AMP Stories, based on technology developed for Google’s Accelerated Mobile Pages project. The Stories, which are already integrated into mobile Google Search, are meant to give Google its own alternative to the Stories offering found in other apps like Snapchat, Instagram and Facebook. But, in Google’s case, Stories are focused on publisher content.
   Today, Google is referring to this visual content as “Web Stories,” not AMP Stories, and is integrating the experience into the Google search app in the U.S., India and Brazil to start.
  Here, users will see the row of Web Stories at the top of the Discover tab, where they can tap to enter the full-screen Story experience. Like Stories found in other apps, you can tap to move forward to the next page in a Google Web Story or swipe to move to a different Story in the carousel.
  Publishers are responsible for authoring their Stories and have control over Story monetization, hosting and sharing, and adding links to the Stories, Google notes. To create these Stories, the publishers can use drag-and-drop tools like the  Web Story editor for WordPress,  MakeStories or  NewsroomAI. Technical users can instead opt to code Web Stories themselves.
 Early adopters of the format have been using Web Stories on their home page, on social channels, in newsletters and more, in addition to having them featured in Google Search, the company says.
  Google has been working with a number of publishers to help them create Web Stories for Search and now, its native mobile app. Partners include Forbes, Vice, Refinery29, USA Today, Lonely Planet, Now This, Thrillist, PopSugar, The Dodo, Bustle, Input, Nylon, The Hollywood Reporter, Blavity, PC Gamer, Golfweek and many others. The publishers and Google collaborated on the new product and helped build out its features, Google says.
 To date, more than 2,000 websites have published Stories that have been indexed by Google.
title: 谷歌在其Google iOS和Android应用程序中添加了故事功能，这是一个专注于发布者内容的旋转木马，可以包括音频、全屏视频和照片
title_en: Google adds a Stories feature to its Google iOS and Android app, a carousel focusing on publisher content that can include audio, full-screen video, and photos
